title: Run Error Handling Flow
interaction_flow: Runner/run_loop → build_run_error_data(input, new_items, raw_responses, last_agent, reasoning_item_id_policy) → RunErrorData
build_run_error_data: loops new_items → run_item_to_input_item(item, policy) → converted items
build_run_error_data: returns RunErrorData{input, new_items, history, output, raw_responses, last_agent}
resolve_run_error_handler_result → RunErrorHandler(user callback): handler(RunErrorHandlerInput{error, context, run_data})
resolve_run_error_handler_result: normalises result → RunErrorHandlerResult (or None if handler returns None / not registered)
resolve_run_error_handler_result: dict with 'final_output' key → RunErrorHandlerResult(**result); raw value → RunErrorHandlerResult(final_output=value)
[handler returns None OR no handler]: Runner raises MaxTurnsExceeded (non-streamed) OR enqueues QueueCompleteSentinel (streamed)
validate_handler_final_output → get_output_schema(agent); validates via AgentOutputSchema._type_adapter.dump_json + output_schema.validate_json; raises UserError on invalid
[include_in_history=True]: Runner appends synthesized_item to generated_items / new_items / session_items
Runner/run_loop: constructs RunResult(final_output=validated_output, ...) and returns normally

[AgentsException propagates]: except-block sets exc.run_data = RunErrorDetails{input, new_items, raw_responses, last_agent, context_wrapper, input_guardrail_results, output_guardrail_results} then re-raises

data_structures.RunErrorData: Snapshot for handler: {input, new_items: list[RunItem], history: list[TResponseInputItem], output: list[TResponseInputItem], raw_responses: list[ModelResponse], last_agent: Agent}
data_structures.RunErrorHandlerInput: Generic[TContext] dataclass: {error: MaxTurnsExceeded, context: RunContextWrapper[TContext], run_data: RunErrorData}
data_structures.RunErrorDetails: Exception payload: {input, new_items, raw_responses, last_agent, context_wrapper, input_guardrail_results, output_guardrail_results}; __str__ calls pretty_print_run_error_details()

key_relationships: resolve_run_error_handler_result invokes user-supplied RunErrorHandler callback (sync or async)
MaxTurnsExceeded extends AgentsException; AgentsException carries run_data: RunErrorDetails | None
RunErrorData is internal snapshot (error_handlers module); RunErrorDetails is the exception payload (exceptions module) — both capture similar run state but serve different roles
create_message_output_item wraps synthesized output in ResponseOutputMessage with FAKE_RESPONSES_ID
branching_logic: no error_handlers OR 'max_turns' key missing → handler skipped, MaxTurnsExceeded raised (non-streamed) or QueueCompleteSentinel enqueued (streamed)
handler returns None → same as no handler (max_turns_handled flag reset in streamed path)
handler returns dict with 'final_output' key → coerced to RunErrorHandlerResult; extra keys → UserError
include_in_history=True → synthesized item appended to history/session/state
include_in_history=False → item NOT appended; run completes with final_output but history unchanged
validate_handler_final_output: plain text agent → passthrough; structured output → serialize+validate via AgentOutputSchema; failure → UserError
important_notes: Error handlers are keyed by kind string ('max_turns' is currently the only supported key). Handlers may be sync or async. RunErrorData vs RunErrorDetails: RunErrorData is the forward-passed snapshot for the handler callback; RunErrorDetails is attached to the re-raised AgentsException for caller inspection. The synthesized MessageOutputItem uses FAKE_RESPONSES_ID to signal it is SDK-generated, not from a real model response.
